rows: Initial SDS baseline derived from the current React/Express/MongoDB implementation.

Reconciled the SDS with the current repository, especially direct password login, password-reset OTP, API inventory, security controls, and implementation risks. Diagram images were intentionally left unchanged.

records.text: * A - Added; M - Modified; D - Deleted
Appendix C. Known Implementation Discrepancies and Risks	31

UNI-MATE is a full-stack web application for students aged 18 to under 30 to discover compatible people, form mutual matches, communicate, create small groups, choose cafes, and use partner vouchers. The reviewed baseline consists of a React/Vite client, an Express/TypeScript API, MongoDB persistence through Mongoose, and Socket.IO realtime messaging.

rows: Implementation baseline: This SDS documents the code present in the repository on 21 July 2026. It describes actual behavior, including direct password login for every role, password-reset OTP, the current mutual-like chat behavior, and the remaining cafe proposal workflow.

records.text: Figure 1. UNI-MATE logical architecture and external integrations

The client uses REST for transactional operations and Socket.IO for realtime chat, typing indicators, read receipts, match updates, and notifications. The backend applies JWT authentication, role checks, Zod validation, rate limiting, and centralized error handling before invoking controllers and services.
Figure 2. Frontend and backend package dependencies

HTTP endpoint declarations and composition of authentication/validation middleware.
Request orchestration, authorization-sensitive checks, response mapping, and socket emission.
Authentication, compatibility scoring, distance routing, place suggestion, email, and notification logic.
Mongoose schemas, constraints, references, indexes, and lifecycle fields.
Authenticated private/group realtime messaging and read/typing events.
JWT authentication, admin authorization, validation, not-found, and error handling.
Zod request contracts for authentication, users, matching, messages, and safety.
Environment configuration, token helpers, zodiac calculation, seeding, and utility functions.

records.text: HTTP routes use resource-oriented paths under /api and JSON request/response bodies.
Protected routes use Bearer access tokens; refresh tokens are also stored in an HTTP-only cookie.
Mongoose ObjectId references model cross-collection relationships; selected aggregates embed arrays and value objects for read efficiency.
Dates use server-side Date values and Mongoose timestamps. Geospatial coordinates use GeoJSON [longitude, latitude].
Realtime rooms are named with either the ChatRoom id, group:<groupId>, or user:<userId>.
Status fields implement lifecycle state rather than physical deletion for matches, rooms, groups, places, reports, and vouchers.

MongoDB is the system of record. Mongoose schemas enforce required fields, enumerated statuses, selected uniqueness constraints, TTL behavior for OTP records, and 2dsphere indexes for user and cafe locations.

One current directed decision per user pair.
At most one room per match.

records.text: This section groups classes and runtime interactions by user-visible function. Boundary objects are React pages, control objects are controllers/services/socket handlers, and entity objects are Mongoose models.

This subsystem creates accounts, authenticates every role directly with email and password, refreshes sessions, resets forgotten passwords through an email OTP plus a short-lived reset token, and collects the profile, preferences, and location required for discovery.
Figure 5. Authentication and profile component relationships

rows: email, password -> user/tokens; delegates registration, sets the refresh cookie, and returns the new session.
email, password -> user/tokens for every role; sets the refresh cookie without an OTP or 2FA branch.
email -> generic OTP-sent response after the password-reset OTP service succeeds.
email, six-digit OTP -> short-lived resetToken after OTP validation and consumption.
resetToken, newPassword -> password replacement; rejects reuse of the current password.
Normalizes email, rejects duplicates, hashes the password, creates an emailVerified user, and issues tokens.
Restores expired suspension, checks account state and password, updates login timestamps, and immediately issues tokens.
Requires an existing password account, stores/verifies the latest EmailOtp, consumes success, and signs a 10-minute password-reset token.
Validates token purpose and account state, rejects the old password, hashes the replacement, and clears the refresh-token hash.
Completes the age-gated profile, provides Vietnam-filtered manual geocoding, and persists whitelisted profile/preference updates.

records.text: Figure 6. Direct password login and the email-OTP password-reset flow
Representative operations used by the implementation are shown below. Mongoose translates these operations to MongoDB queries.

Discovery excludes prior swipes, both directions of blocking, inactive/non-user accounts, incomplete onboarding, invalid locations, incompatible age/purpose/gender filters, and users outside the configured radius. Candidates are ranked with route-time distance, cafe style, interests, goals, major preference, vibe, age, recency, and time overlap.
Figure 7. Discovery, match, cafe proposal, and chat control/entity relationships

rows: targetUserId -> like result; emits incoming-like notification when not mutual.
Loads exclusion/filter sets, geospatial candidates, OSRM route metadata, scores, sorts, and limits to 10.
Two profiles and route metadata -> score, reasons, common tags/styles, distance diagnostics.
Upserts directed action; on mutual like creates/upserts Match and active ChatRoom.
Returns users who liked the current user and have not received a response swipe.

records.text: Figure 8. Discovery like/pass decision and actual mutual-match behavior
Representative operations used by the implementation are shown below. Mongoose translates these operations to MongoDB queries.

rows: Actual behavior: Mutual like currently creates an active ChatRoom immediately. This conflicts with the README rule that chat should remain locked until both users confirm the same cafe.

records.text: A participant can retrieve suggested cafes, replace the active proposal, and notify the other participant. The proposal creator is automatically recorded as the first confirmer. Only the other participant can confirm or reject. Confirmation upserts an active room; rejection clears the selection; cancellation archives an existing room.
Figure 9. MatchController relationships to Match, CafeProposal, PlaceCache, and ChatRoom

rows: matchId -> suggested active cafes; rejects missing/expired match.
matchId/placeId -> proposal; replaces active proposal and sets cafe_proposed.
Adds non-proposer to confirmedBy, accepts proposal, upserts room, and sets chat_opened.
Non-proposer rejects; clears selection/confirmations and returns match to matched.
Sets cancelled, rejects active proposals, archives room, and notifies peer.
Uses participant locations and cached/seeded places to return cafe candidates.

Private chat supports room listing/hiding, chat history, text and file messages, typing indicators, delivery broadcasts, read receipts, and per-recipient notifications. Both HTTP and Socket.IO message submission paths enforce active room and chat_opened match state.
Figure 11. ChatController and SocketHandler relationships to room and message entities

rows: Returns rooms containing user and not hidden by that user, ordered by lastMessageAt.
Verifies membership and states, calculates bilateral block state, and returns up to 100 messages.
HTTP fallback: validates message, persists it, updates room, broadcasts, and notifies peer.
Joins room only when the socket user is a participant and room is active.
Adds reader id to readBy for all room messages and emits message_read.
Persists Notification then emits notification:new to user:<id>.

Users can create purpose-oriented groups, add members by email, remove members or leave, dissolve a group, and exchange realtime or HTTP-fallback group messages. Owner, capacity, membership, and active-state rules are enforced in controllers/socket handlers.
Figure 13. Group management and group-chat components

rows: Creates group with requester as creator and first member; default capacity is six.
Lists active groups containing the requester and populates creator/members.
Owner-only; validates capacity and active target user found by email.
Owner may remove others; member may leave; owner cannot leave without dissolving.
Verifies member and returns group history.
Validates active membership, persists GroupMessage, broadcasts, and notifies peers.

Authenticated users browse active cafes and available vouchers. A prospective owner submits one pending partner-place application; admin approval activates the place and upgrades the owner role to partner. Partners manage owned place data and voucher lifecycle. Users save valid vouchers atomically against quota.
Figure 15. Places, partner ownership, and voucher component relationships

rows: Validates cafe form/hours and creates one pending partner place per applicant.
Returns active, unexpired, non-exhausted vouchers and savedByMe flag.
Idempotent save; atomically adds user and increments usage only while valid.
Lists owned places and updates whitelisted fields after ownership check.
Requires partner ownership, active cafe, required values, and unique code within cafe.
Owner-scoped voucher activation toggle or deletion.
Transitions place status, upgrades partner role on approval, notifies owner, and audits.

Representative operations used by the implementation are shown below. Mongoose translates these operations to MongoDB queries.

Safety operations validate report context, prioritize repeated independent reports, block communication across related matches/rooms, and restore communication only when neither party continues blocking. Admin moderation supports dismiss, warn, suspend, and ban with warning-count escalation and audit logging.
Figure 17. Safety and moderation control/entity relationships

rows: Creates report with optional match/room/message/evidence and marks priority after three distinct reporters.
Adds target to blockedUsers and sets all pair Matches/ChatRooms to blocked.
Removes block; restores chat_opened/active only if target does not still block requester.
Loads report context and up to 200 room messages for investigation.
Applies one terminal action, updates account/report, emits notice, disconnects locked user.

Admin pages provide dashboard counts, user search/detail/update/status control, report queue/detail/resolution, match search, place review/update/delete/status control, and audit log listing. All admin routes require authentication and the admin role.

Filters users and loads selected user's match/report history.
Admin-managed account creation and full editable profile/role/status update.
Transitions active/suspended/banned and records audit reason.
Filters by status or searches participant, cafe, object id, and status text.
Review and lifecycle management of cafe records.

rows: Short-lived JWT access token and refresh token hash/cookie. Registration and login issue tokens directly after password validation for user, partner, and admin roles.
requireAuth on protected routers; requireAdmin and requirePartner on privileged areas; ownership/member checks inside controllers.
bcrypt password and OTP hashes. EmailOtp is expiring, consumable, resend/attempt-limited, and currently used only for forgot-password; login has no OTP/2FA enforcement.
Zod request validation; Mongoose enum/range constraints; upload type/size checks.
Helmet, CORS with configured client origin, 2 MB JSON limit, cookie parser, and 500 requests per 15-minute rate limit.
Suspended/banned/inactive users cannot log in; moderated sockets are disconnected.
Password and refresh hashes excluded from normal/admin projections; report context restricted to authorized admin routes.
Verify private-room membership and active state, then join room id.
Persist and broadcast private message; notify other participant.
Verify active group membership and join group:<id>.
Persist/broadcast group message and notify other members.
Persist group readBy and emit receipt.
Mutual like currently creates chat_opened directly.
Only one active proposal is intended per match.
Cancel archives; bilateral unblock may reactivate.
Active partner place upgrades owner to partner.
Controller currently directly uses dismissed/resolved_valid for final actions.
active -> suspended/banned; suspended -> active after expiry
Failure is logged; distance score falls back to zero.
Cached or seeded fallback places keep the flow testable.
Non-2xx returns 502; results are filtered to Vietnam/city.
Development may print OTP when configured; service errors propagate through the centralized handler.
20 MB chat limit; 5 MB image-only report evidence.

records.text: All routes below are prefixed with /api. Except for register, login, forgot-password OTP/reset, and refresh, routes require an authenticated access token. Admin and partner groups apply additional role checks.

rows: Create account, mark email verified, and issue tokens.
Validate password and issue tokens directly for every role.

records.text: Frontend: React, TypeScript, Vite, React Router, Zustand, Axios, Tailwind CSS, Socket.IO Client.
Backend: Node.js, Express, TypeScript, Mongoose, JWT, bcryptjs, Zod, Socket.IO, Multer, Nodemailer.
Database: MongoDB with geospatial and TTL indexes.
Development entry points: frontend http://localhost:5173 and backend http://localhost:5000.

rows: JWT access/refresh secrets and token lifetimes; cookie secure flag. The access secret also signs 10-minute password-reset tokens.
SMTP host, port, credentials, sender, and development OTP logging for the forgot-password flow.
Google Maps/Places API key or cached/seeded fallback.
OSRM endpoint and scoring thresholds/weights/candidate limit.
Writable uploads directory and public /uploads route.

records.text: Provide backend and frontend environment files.
Install backend dependencies and start MongoDB or configure MongoDB Atlas.
Install frontend dependencies and run the Vite client.
Verify /health, authentication, protected REST routes, and Socket.IO connectivity.
Appendix C. Known Implementation Discrepancies and Risks

rows: Mutual like creates Match(status=chat_opened) and active ChatRoom immediately, contrary to the README and admin setting cafeGateRequired=true.
Cafe state is overwritten by read endpoints

selectPlace sets cafe_proposed, but listMatches/getMatch call openChatForMatch and can immediately restore chat_opened. If that happens before confirmation, the active proposal may remain unaccepted and the room may not receive the selected cafe.

Current login issues tokens directly for admin accounts, the general /auth/send-otp and /auth/verify-otp endpoints and User.twoFactorEnabled field are removed, while README and /admin/settings still claim admin 2FA is required.
Registration sets emailVerified=true without an OTP. EmailOtp is currently used only for forgot-password and does not verify registration or login.
Match.users is an unrestricted array; the schema does not enforce exactly two users.
Tag has its own catalogue model but no active admin route/controller manages it; PlaceCache stores tag names as strings and does not reference Tag ids.
Unblock restores all blocked pair matches to chat_opened, even if their prior state was not an open chat.
Both Otp and EmailOtp exist; the current auth service uses EmailOtp only for forgot-password, while Otp is unused by active routes.
Uploads are stored on the application host and require persistent/shared storage for scaled deployment.

Recommended decisions: Either enforce cafe confirmation before creating ChatRoom, or formally remove the cafe-gated requirement and stop moving chat_opened matches back to cafe_proposed. Separately, either restore enforced admin 2FA or update README and /admin/settings so the published policy matches the login implementation.
